返回本批次

显示 HN：代理内核 – 三个 Markdown 文件，使任何 AI 代理都有状态

Show HN: Agent Kernel – Three Markdown files that make any AI agent stateful

github.com 作者 obilgic 分数 39 评论 19 2026/03/23 15:06
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 创建 AI 代理的最简单方法。克隆、开始、说话。您的代理会在会议之间进行记忆、做笔记并以过去的工作为基础。没有框架，没有数据库——只有三个 Markdown 文件和一个 git 存储库。适用于任何 AI 编码代理：OpenCode、Claude Code、Codex、Cursor、Windsurf 等。 快速启动 git clone https://github.com/oguzbilgic/agent-kernel.git my-agent cd my-agent 启动您的代理：opencode # 或 claude、codex、cursor 等。就这样。代理读取内核，意识到它是新的，询问你想要它是谁。你告诉它。它记得。内存结构 AGENTS.md ← 内核（通用，不要编辑） IDENTITY.md ← 这个代理是谁（代理维护） KNOWLEDGE.md ← 知识文件索引（代理维护） 知识/ ← 关于世界的事实（可变） 笔记/ ← 每日会话日志（仅附加）
  2. 两种记忆：
  3. 知识/——状态。关于现在情况的事实。当现实发生变化时，代理会更新这些。笔记/——叙述。每次会议发生了什么——决定、行动、未决项目。仅追加。当日结束后不再修改。
  4. 为什么这有效 AI 代理已经将 AGENTS.md（或 CLAUDE.md、.cursorrules 等）读取为项目指令。该内核使用该机制来教导智能体如何记忆。该代理不需要数据库、矢量存储或自定义框架。它只需要：
  5. 一个文件，上面写着“你是有状态的，这就是如何”一个 git 存储库，用于在普通 markdown 文件中存储内存

Original Summary

  1. The easiest way to create an AI agent. Clone, start, talk. Your agent remembers between sessions, takes notes, and builds on past work. No framework, no database — just three markdown files and a git repo. Works with any AI coding agent: OpenCode, Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, etc. Quick start git clone https://github.com/oguzbilgic/agent-kernel.git my-agent cd my-agent Start your agent: opencode # or claude, codex, cursor, etc. That's it. The agent reads the kernel, realizes it's new, asks who you want it to be. You tell it. It remembers. Memory structure AGENTS.md ← kernel (generic, don't edit) IDENTITY.md ← who this agent is (agent maintains) KNOWLEDGE.md ← index of knowledge files (agent maintains) knowledge/ ← facts about the world (mutable) notes/ ← daily session logs (append-only)
  2. Two kinds of memory:
  3. knowledge/ — State. Facts about how things are right now. The agent updates these when reality changes. notes/ — Narrative. What happened each session — decisions, actions, open items. Append-only. Never modified after the day ends.
  4. Why this works AI agents already read AGENTS.md (or CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, etc.) as project instructions. This kernel uses that mechanism to teach the agent how to remember. The agent doesn't need a database, a vector store, or a custom framework. It just needs:
  5. A file that says "you are stateful, here's how" A git repo to store memory in Plain markdown files

评论（默认中文）

bigbezet 2026/03/23 18:16
许多开发人员，包括我在内，已经尝试过类似的方法。我真的不认为这种方法可靠。

首先，像 Claude Code 这样的工具已经具备了自动记忆功能。

其次，我想我们现在都知道代理并不总是可靠地遵循 AGENTS.md 文件中的指令，特别是当上下文大小增加时。如果我们想保证某些事情发生，我们应该使用钩子。

已经有解决方案可以跟踪代理所做的事情，甚至在不影响代理上下文窗口的情况下对其进行总结。 Claude Code 等工具以可分析的方式记录活动，因此您可以使用工具来处理它。

当我过去尝试类似的方法时，代理不会真正理解 KNOWLEDGE.md 文件中“记住”什么是重要的，并且会产生大量的膨胀，然后我无论如何都需要清理它们。

有现有的工具可以告诉代理最近发生了什么：git。通过查看提交消息和更改文件列表，代理通常会获取所需的大部分信息。如果有任何非常重要的决定或学习需要代理了解更多，那么它们应该由开发人员>手动<写下来，因为我不相信代理会做出决定。

此外，关于是否需要 AGENTS.md 文件，或者是否应该将它们保持在绝对最小值，目前正在进行讨论。不管我们最初的想法如何，根据最近的研究，这些文件实际上会对输出产生负面影响。

A lot of devs, including me, have tried something similar already. I don't really find this approach reliable.

Firstly, tools like Claude Code already have things like auto memory.

Secondly, I think we all learned by now that agents will not always reliably follow instructions in the AGENTS.md file, especially as the size of the context increases. If we want to guarantee that something happens, we should use hooks.

There are already solutions to track what the agent did and even summarising it without affecting the context window of the agent. Tools like Claude Code log activity in a way which is possible to analyze, so you can use tools to process that.

When I tried something similar in the past, the agent would not really understand what is important to "memorise" in a KNOWLEDGE.md file, and would create a lot of bloat which I would then need to clean up anyway.

There are existing tools to tell the agent what has happened recently: git. By looking at the commit messages and list of changed files, the agent usually gets most of the information it needs. If there are any very important decisions or learnings which are necessary for the agent to understand more, they should be written down >manually< by a developer, as I don't trust the agent to decide that.

Also, there is an ongoing discussion about whether AGENTS.md files are even needed, or whether they should be kept to an absolute minimum. Despite what we all initially thought, those files can actually negatively affect the output, based on recent research.

在 HN 中查看
didgeoridoo 2026/03/24 06:18
沿着这些思路，我正在开发一个名为 Spotless[0] 的工具，它采用更基于 HTTP 代理的方法来使代理不必担心状态性。它直接读取和写入来自 Anthropic 的消息数组，因此您不必依赖调用 MCP 或使用技能的代理。仍然存在缺陷并且还处于早期阶段，但这绝对是与代理合作的一种非常有趣的方式。

https://github.com/LabLeaks/spotless

Along these lines, I’m working on a tool called Spotless[0] that takes a more HTTP proxy-based approach to make statefulness something the agent doesn’t have to worry about. It directly reads & writes to the messages array going to and from Anthropic, so you don’t have to rely on the agent calling an MCP or using a skill. Still buggy and early, but it’s definitely a very interesting way of working with agents.

https://github.com/LabLeaks/spotless

在 HN 中查看
esperent 2026/03/23 17:41
> 笔记/——叙述。每次会议发生了什么——决定、行动、未决项目。仅追加。当日结束后不再修改

我已经必须不断地与代理作斗争，以防止它为我更改或删除的内容添加向后兼容性、解决方法、包装器等。即使有一个被遗忘的评论引用了旧的方式，如果我走出房间 5 分钟，它就会编写一个完整的转换器系统，以防万一我们需要它，即使我的代理文件明确表示不要这样做（YAGNI，所有向后兼容性必须由我清除，未经我明确批准不得使用包装器，等等）。记录我们上个月尝试过但失败并被丢弃的事情听起来是个坏主意，除非它是 具体来说 策划说“失败的事情列表：...”，根据定义，仅附加日志无法做到这一点。

我遇到过这样的情况：它甚至通过 git 历史记录发现了已删除的系统。至少这种情况很少见。

> notes/ — Narrative. What happened each session — decisions, actions, open items. Append-only. Never modified after the day ends

I already have to fight the agent constantly to prevent it adding backwards compatibility, workarounds, wrappers etc. for things that I changed or removed. If there's even one forgotten comment that references the old way, it'll write a whole converter system if I walk out of the room for 5 minutes, just in case we ever need it, and even though my agents file specifically says not to (YAGNI, all backwards compatibility must be cleared by me, no wrappers without my explicit approval, etc.). Having a log of the things we tried last month but which failed and got discarded sounds like a bad idea, unless it's specifically curated to say "list of things that failed: ...", which by definition, an append only log can't do.

I have hit the situation where it discovered removed systems through git history, even. At least that's rare though.

在 HN 中查看
aiboost 2026/03/23 16:41
文本文件 + Git > 矢量数据库。干得好。 我很好奇这是如何扩展的。随着笔记/目录在数周或数月内不断增长，阅读过去的每日日志将耗尽上下文窗口。
Text files + Git > Vector DBs. Nice work. I'm curious about how this scales. As the notes/ directory grows over weeks or months, reading past daily logs will eat up the context window.
在 HN 中查看
mrugge 2026/03/23 17:45
可以添加一个计划的 GitHub 操作，将长期历史记录压缩到矢量数据库中，然后让代理除了 md 和 git 历史记录之外还检查该矢量数据库。
Could add a scheduled GitHub action that compacts long history into a vector db and then have the agents also check that vector db in addition to md and git history.

在 HN 中查看
sathish316 2026/03/23 21:44
一旦您创建多个副本并将每个副本用作专门代理，这会令人惊讶地好。也许我们不需要 OpenClaw 只是使用自然语言来管理电子邮件、日历、松弛、待办事项列表等。

Agent-kernel具有个性、持久记忆、自我修改能力，使用Skills与使用Claude代码中的Skills相同。

This is surprisingly good, once you create multiple copies and use each copy as a specialized agent. Maybe we don't need OpenClaw just to manage email, calendar, slack, todo lists etc using natural language.

Agent-kernel has personality, persistent memory, self-modifying capability, using Skills is same as using Skills from Claude code.

在 HN 中查看
sathish316 2026/03/24 01:50
有关如何使用 AgentKernel 构建自我修改的单一用途代理的说明，请参见此处 - https://x.com/sathish316/status/2036092222953951364?s=46

这类似于将 OpenClaw 用于特定用例，但没有 OpenClaw 的大部分复杂性或安全隐患。

Instructions on How to build a self-modifying single-purpose agent using AgentKernel here - https://x.com/sathish316/status/2036092222953951364?s=46

This is similar to using OpenClaw for a specific usecase, but without most of the complexities or security implications of OpenClaw.

在 HN 中查看
JasperNoboxdev 2026/03/24 00:16
我发现有趣的是，人工智能在编写代理流程方面出奇地糟糕。我们正在建立一个轻量级系统，其中核心逻辑位于 Markdown 文件中，代理身份、规则、上下文、内存用于创建特定的报告输出。

每次我们要求模型帮助构建这个系统时，它都会尝试编写一个 Python 脚本。它无法阻止自己追求“真正的代码”。编排层只是另一个法学硕士读取的结构化文本的想法在某种程度上与它格格不入，尽管它实际上就是这样工作的。

这就像要求一个母语人士教他们的语言，然后他们开始构建 Duolingo

What I find interesting is that AI is surprisingly bad at writing agentic flows. We're setting up a lightweight system where the core logic lives in markdown files agent identity, rules, context, memory to create specific report outputs.

Every time we ask the model to help build out this system, it tries to write a Python script instead. It cannot stop itself from reaching for "real code." The idea that the orchestration layer is just structured text that another LLM reads is somehow alien to it, even though that's literally how it works.

It's like asking a native speaker to teach their language and they start building Duolingo

在 HN 中查看
avereveard 2026/03/23 19:31
具有相同功能的一个文件（可能以代码为中心，易于调整）

---

唯一要编写的文档是project.md 和TODO.md 不要在其他任何地方编写文档。

TODO.md：记录差距、任务和进度，按功能分组

project.md：文档架构、职责地图、功能以及查找事物所需的部落知识

不要记录代码、方法、类。

标准操作程序：

任务开始：

- 阅读：goals.md tech.md project.md

- 更新 TODO.md 在您将实现的 # 功能下添加逐步 [ ] 任务

任务执行过程中：

- 逐步执行任务，如果可能的话委托给子任务或子代理

- 使用 [x] 记录在 TODO.md 中执行的工作

任务结束：

- 从 TODO.md 中删除已完成的功能

- 维护项目.md

the one file that does the same (maybe code focused, easily adapted)

---

Only documentation to write is project.md and TODO.md do not write documentation anywhere else.

TODO.md: document gaps, tasks and progress, grouped by feature

project.md: document architecture, responsability map, features and the tribal knowledge needed to find things

Do not document code, method, classes.

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURES:

Beginning of task:

- read: goals.md tech.md project.md

- update TODO.md add step by step [ ] tasks under the # feature you will implement

During execution of task:

- perform the task step by step, delegate if possible to sub tasks or sub agents

- log with [x] the work performed in TODO.md as you go

End of task:

- remove completed features from the TODO.md

- maintain project.md

在 HN 中查看
knollimar 2026/03/23 21:33
这不会导致子代理不记录他们的工作吗？
Does this not result in subagents not logging their work?
在 HN 中查看
avereveard 2026/03/24 00:38
我更喜欢协调者根据子代理的回答有发言权
I prefer the orchestrator to have a say, based on the answer of the sub agent
在 HN 中查看
knollimar 2026/03/24 01:16
子代理如何知道要记什么样的笔记？编排器是否会直接丢弃它们或进行自我总结？

我发现这种交互是我的子代理想法爆发的地方（没有上下传输足够的数据）

How does the subagent know what kind of notes to take? Does fhe orchestrator just discard them or summarize itself?

I find this interaction is where my subagent ideas explode (not transferring enough data up and down)

在 HN 中查看
avereveard 2026/03/24 02:37
大多数子代理工具中都有子代理调用的工具，以通知完成要返回的消息以及有关如何返回内容的良好文档，以便协调器可以进行编排。

没有该机制的安全带可以通过提示来增强它

roo code 有一个很好的工具（但是对代理来说默认提示很糟糕），kiro 也做得很好，gemini 有一些失误，因为它只是发送东西而不真正关心默认的回报（尽管非常依赖模型），同时 jules 使用相同的模型有一个很好的工具和良好的反馈循环。

most subagent tool have in the tool the sub agent calls to notify completion the message to return and a good documentation about how to return things, so the orchesrtator can orchestrate.

the harness that don't have that mechanism can have it augmented by prompt

roo code has a good harness for that (but terrible default prompts for the agents), kiro does that well too, gemini has a few misses as it just send off things without really caring of returns by default (albeit very model dependent), meanwhile jules using the same models has a good harness with a good feedback loop.

在 HN 中查看
_pdp_ 2026/03/23 18:24
> AI 代理已将 AGENTS.md（或 CLAUDE.md、.cursorrules 等）读取为项目指令。该内核使用该机制来教导智能体如何记忆。

老兄，这只是提示。它与要求克劳德代码自己编写这些文件一样有用。

> AI agents already read AGENTS.md (or CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, etc.) as project instructions. This kernel uses that mechanism to teach the agent how to remember.

Dude, this is just prompts. It is as useful as asking claude code to write these files itself.

在 HN 中查看
gaigalas 2026/03/23 19:06
令人好奇的是，特工们会记住创伤性事件并重演它们，而不是回避它们。

我被一项任务困住了几天。删除了一些调试会话的内存，事情又重新解锁了。安全带基本上是一遍又一遍地重播创伤。

老实说，我认为与代理合作时最好不要有状态的东西。

It's curious when agents remember traumatic events and replay them instead of avoiding them.

I was stuck on a task for a couple of days. Deleted the memory about some debugging sessions, thing just unlocked itself again. The harness was basically replaying the trauma over and over again.

I honestly think it's better to not have stateful stuff when working with agents.

在 HN 中查看
AgentTax 2026/03/24 00:43
具有讽刺意味的是，出于合规性目的，您几乎需要状态性 - 600 美元的 1099-NEC 阈值或 100K 美元的关联阈值只有在有人跟踪跨会话的累积总数时才有意义。临时代理在架构上更简洁，但它们会产生合规性盲点。
The irony is that for compliance purposes you almost need statefulness — a $600 1099-NEC threshold or a $100K nexus threshold only makes sense if someone's tracking cumulative totals across sessions. Ephemeral agents are cleaner architecturally but they create a compliance blind spot.
在 HN 中查看
bavell 2026/03/23 22:02
我发现记忆和状态与法学硕士混杂在一起。到目前为止，我并不关心长期记忆 - 通常只有短期或中期会话日志或以任务为中心的文档。
I've found memories and state to be a mixed bag with LLMs. To the point I don't bother with long term memories - usually only short or medium-term session logs or task-focused docs.
在 HN 中查看
jamiemallers 2026/03/23 23:05 deleted
[死的]
[dead]
在 HN 中查看
chrisdudek 2026/03/23 20:07
我已经作为秘书与代理人一起工作了 3-4 周。 CLAUDE.md，每日日志，状态文件，管道跟踪。

bigbezet 是对的，特工不知道什么值得记住。对我来说有效的方法是将其分开：代理记录发生的事情，我决定真正重要的事情。需要管理的两个地方：日志和 STATE.MD，我根据我的期望要求维护它们。如果需要，代理可以读取日志，但检查状态的主要位置是 STATE.md。

不过，有一件事我还没有看到有人提到过。经过几周的阅读你对某个同事的咆哮后，代理人在所有事情上都站在你一边。必须将“考虑他人的观点”逐字添加到我的规则文件中。只是日记里有太多片面的注释。否则你最终会遇到一个记忆力完美的唯唯诺诺的人。

盖加拉斯提到的创伤重放也是真实的。我发现很难不让代理人有偏见。坦率地说，即使我也注意到这样的事情： - 我抱怨，特工为我辩护。 - 我正在聊天中输入其他法学硕士的回复，该回复并没有受到我的日记的影响。它翻转了立场，现在说这项研究很有意义。 - 我说：“你现在有多少偏见。”它回应了一些关于偏见和“……坦率地说，事实是：……”。 即使要求不存在偏见，它也会开始表现出偏见，因为它认为我期望如此。狡猾的混蛋。

I've been working with an agent as a secretary for 3-4 weeks now. CLAUDE.md, daily journal, state file, pipeline tracking.

bigbezet is right, agents have no clue what's worth remembering. What works for me is splitting it: the agent writes what happened, I decide what actually matters. Two places to manage: journal and the STATE.MD, which I request to maintain based on my expectations. Agent can read a journal if it needs, but the main place to check the status is STATE.md.

One thing I haven't seen anyone mention, though. After a few weeks of reading your rants about some coworker, the agent just takes your side on everything. Had to literally add "consider the other person's perspective" to my rules file. It just has too many one-sided notes in the journal. Otherwise you end up with a yes-man that has perfect memory.

The trauma replay thing gaigalas mentioned is real too. I found it hard to not make agent be biased. To be frank, even I'm noticing something like this: - I complain, agent defends me. - I'm putting into the chat a response from other llm which was not biased by my journal. It flips sides and now says the research makes much sense. - I say: "How much biased you are right now." and it responds something about being biased and "... to be frank, the truth is: ...". Even when asking for not being biased, is starts to play biased because it thinks I expect that. Sneaky bastard.

在 HN 中查看
renewiltord 2026/03/23 18:20
是的，所有基于爪的特工都使用类似的结构。我对带有嵌入功能的 SQLite DB 进行了一些实验，以便它可以进行矢量搜索，但我没有设法让它做得更好。最好的方法仍然是将内容放在上下文中并让它全文搜索历史记录。
Yeah, all the claw based agents use similar structure. I experimented a little with SQLite DB with embedding so it could vector search but I did not manage to get it to do better. Best is still to just stuff things in context and let it full text search history.
